page: Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Gen9 processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 microarchitecture running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9g.8xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test registers a strong score of 64161.3, while single-core performance sits in the average tier. Memory subsystem performance is notable, with PassMark memory scores and cached read-only memory bandwidth reaching top-tier classifications. Data processing benchmarks show top-tier results for single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression.

The instance exhibits some performance tradeoffs, particularly in LLM inference tasks where text generation for a small 135M model is classified as weak, though prompt processing remains average. However, database operations and web serving are highly efficient, with PassMark database operations scoring in the top tier and static web serving throughput reaching 92.63 GB/sec. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for database hosting, web serving, and general-purpose multi-threaded workloads, offering high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency due to its dedicated ARMv9 cores and high memory bandwidth.
description: The Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the ARMv9-based AWS Graviton Gen9 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 128.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, alongside exceptional throughput in database operations and static web serving. However, it shows weaker performance in specific LLM text generation tasks. The m9g.8xlarge offers a balanced resource profile, making it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated Graviton Gen9 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton Gen9 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core ARMv9 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth, database throughput, and balanced resource density for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores running at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators
